requirements.title: User Registration
requirements.description: The system shall allow a new user to register an account by providing a valid email address and other necessary details.
requirements.rationale: To enable users to securely access the platform, they need to create an account first.
requirements.business_value: Enhances user engagement and accessibility on the platform, leading to increased user retention and satisfaction.

source.description: As a new user I need to register an account so that I can securely access the platform.

acceptance_criteria: Given I am a new user on the registration page
When I submit my registration details with a valid email address
Then I create my account
postconditions: The system creates a new user account in the database
A confirmation email is sent to the provided email address for verification
The user is redirected to the login page with a success message
functional_constraints: User must provide an email address that is not already registered
The system should validate the email format and ensure uniqueness
security_requirements: The registration process should be secure against SQL injection and other common web vulnerabilities
User data, including emails, should be encrypted both at rest and in transit
A mechanism for account verification must be implemented (e.g., email confirmation)

non_functional_requirements.performance: The registration process should complete within 2 seconds
non_functional_requirements.availability: The system should have a 99.9% uptime during peak hours
non_functional_requirements.scalability: The system should handle at least 1,000 new user registrations per minute without performance degradation
non_functional_requirements.usability: Error messages should be clear and actionable, guiding the user on how to correct their input

edge_cases: What happens if the user provides an email address that is already registered?
How does the system handle invalid email formats?
What is the behavior when the network connection is poor during registration?

interfaces.name: User Database
interfaces.description: The requirement interacts with the User Database to check for existing user accounts and store new ones.

interfaces.name: Email Notification System
interfaces.description: The requirement uses the Email Notification System to send a confirmation email to the registered user's email address.

test_cases.description: User attempts to register with a valid email address.
test_cases.expected_result: The system creates a new user account and sends a confirmation email. The user is redirected to the login page.

test_cases.description: User attempts to register with an already registered email address.
test_cases.expected_result: The system displays an error message informing the user that the email address is already in use.

test_cases.description: User attempts to register with an invalid email format.
test_cases.expected_result: The system displays an error message indicating that the email format is incorrect.

test_cases.description: Network connection is poor during registration.
test_cases.expected_result: The system completes the registration process but sends a notification to the user about potential issues with their network connection.

requirements.title: Soft Delete User Account
requirements.description: The system shall allow a user to request the soft deletion of their account, marking it as deleted and preserving the data for a grace period.
requirements.rationale: To provide users with a way to preserve their data during a transition, allowing for potential recovery or data restoration before permanent deletion.
requirements.business_value: Improves user satisfaction by giving them more control over their data while ensuring compliance with data retention policies.

source.description: As a user, I need to soft-delete my account so that my data is preserved for a grace period while my account is marked as deleted.

acceptance_criteria: Account is marked as deleted
Data is preserved for a grace period
preconditions: User has an active account
functional_constraints: Soft delete operation must not permanently remove the account immediately
security_requirements: No access to deleted data by unauthorized users during grace period

non_functional_requirements.availability: Account should remain accessible during the grace period
non_functional_requirements.usability: User interface should clearly indicate that account is soft-deleted and provide instructions on how to recover or permanently delete account

edge_cases: User attempts to soft-delete their account while already in a grace period
User tries to recover data before the grace period ends without providing necessary information

interfaces.name: Account Management API
interfaces.description: The requirement interacts with this API to update account status and manage data retention.

interfaces.name: User Data Database
interfaces.description: The requirement depends on this database to store and retrieve user data during the grace period.

test_cases.description: User requests soft deletion while not in a grace period.
test_cases.expected_result: Account is marked as deleted and data is preserved for the configured grace period.

test_cases.description: User tries to recover data before the grace period ends.
test_cases.expected_result: Data recovery process starts successfully, and user is prompted to provide necessary information.

test_cases.description: Grace period ends without user action.
test_cases.expected_result: Account is permanently deleted, and data is removed from the system.

requirements.title: Implement paginated results for feeds and search results
requirements.description: The system shall display results in a paginated manner, with each page showing a reasonable number of items (e.g., 20). Users shall be able to navigate between pages using next/previous links or page numbers.
requirements.rationale: To improve user experience by reducing long loading times when browsing feeds or search results.
requirements.business_value: Enhanced usability and satisfaction, leading to higher engagement with the application.

source.description: As a user I need paginated results when browsing feeds or search results So that to avoid long loading times

acceptance_criteria: I see a 'Load More' button or page navigation controls
Only the first page of results is loaded initially
preconditions: I am browsing a feed or viewing search results
functional_constraints: Pagination applies to both feeds and search result pages
Each page shows a reasonable number of items (e.g., 20)

non_functional_requirements.usability: Error handling for cases where there are no more pages to load

edge_cases: What happens when there is an error during the pagination process?
How does the system handle cases where the number of items on a page changes dynamically?
What if the user tries to navigate to a non-existent page?

interfaces.name: Feed and Search Result Pages
interfaces.description: The requirement interacts with the UI components responsible for displaying the paginated results.

interfaces.name: Data Fetching API
interfaces.description: The requirement interacts with an API to fetch data for pagination. The API must support fetching a subset of items based on page number and item count.

test_cases.description: Verify that the 'Load More' button or page navigation controls are displayed when browsing a feed or viewing search results.
test_cases.expected_result: The 'Load More' button or page navigation controls are visible.

test_cases.description: Verify that only the first page of results is loaded initially when browsing a feed or viewing search results.
test_cases.expected_result: Only the first page of results is displayed, and no further items are loaded.

test_cases.description: Verify that navigation to subsequent pages works correctly when browsing a feed or viewing search results.
test_cases.expected_result: Users can navigate between pages using next/previous links or page numbers, and the correct set of items is displayed on each page.

test_cases.description: Verify that error handling works correctly when there are no more pages to load.
test_cases.expected_result: Error message or UI indication is shown to the user indicating that there are no more pages available.

requirements.title: User can create a text post and optionally upload images
requirements.description: The system shall allow a logged-in user to create a new post containing text content and optionally upload one or more images, which will then be saved and shared with other users.
requirements.rationale: This requirement is necessary to enable users to share their thoughts and media with others, enhancing community engagement and interaction.
requirements.business_value: Increasing user engagement and content sharing can lead to higher user retention and a more vibrant social environment within the platform.

source.description: As a user I need to create a text post and optionally upload images so that I can share content with others.

acceptance_criteria: A logged-in user can create a new post containing text content.
A logged-in user can optionally upload one or more images to the post.
The post is saved and shared with other users.
preconditions: The user must be logged in.
postconditions: The post is successfully created and saved.
The post is visible to other users.
security_requirements: Authentication of the user before creating a post.
Encryption of any uploaded images for data security.

non_functional_requirements.performance: The system must respond within 2 seconds when creating and saving a post with text content and images.
non_functional_requirements.availability: The system must be available 99.9% of the time.
non_functional_requirements.scalability: The system must handle at least 1,000 concurrent users without performance degradation.
non_functional_requirements.usability: Error messages should be clear and user-friendly.

edge_cases: User tries to create a post without any content or images.
System fails to save a post due to network issues.
A user attempts to upload an image that exceeds the file size limit.
dependencies: Authentication system for verifying user identity.
Image processing service for handling uploaded images.

interfaces.name: Post Creation API
interfaces.description: Interface used to receive and process post creation requests from the UI or other services.

interfaces.name: Content Database
interfaces.description: Stores information about posts, including text content and image metadata.

interfaces.name: Image Storage Service
interfaces.description: Used for storing uploaded images securely and making them accessible to other users.

test_cases.description: User creates a post with text content only.
test_cases.expected_result: The post is successfully created, saved in the database, and visible to other users.

test_cases.description: User creates a post with both text content and images.
test_cases.expected_result: The post is successfully created, saved in the database, and visible to other users. Images are stored securely and linked to the post.

test_cases.description: User tries to create a post without any content or images.
test_cases.expected_result: The system rejects the request and displays an error message indicating that both text content and/or images are required.

requirements.title: Enable/Disable Notifications per Category
requirements.description: The system shall allow users to enable or disable notifications per category such as likes, comments, and friend requests.
requirements.rationale: Enabling users to control which notifications they receive provides a personalized experience and reduces unnecessary alerts, improving user satisfaction and engagement.
requirements.business_value: Improves user satisfaction by allowing them to tailor their notification preferences, potentially leading to higher app retention rates and more active participation in the application's features.

source.description: As a user I need to enable or disable notifications per category (e.g., likes, comments, friend requests) So that I can control which notifications I receive

acceptance_criteria: Given I am logged into the application When I navigate to the notification settings Then I see a list of notification categories with toggles
Given I am on the notification settings page When I toggle off "likes" notifications Then I no longer receive notifications for likes
Given I am on the notification settings page When I toggle on "comments" notifications Then I receive notifications for comments
preconditions: The user is logged into the application.
functional_constraints: Each category has a toggle (on/off).
Changes take effect immediately.
Supported notification categories include likes, comments, and friend requests.
edge_cases: What happens if a user tries to toggle a notification category that is not supported by the system?
How does the system handle changes when multiple users are toggling different categories simultaneously?

interfaces.name: Notification Settings Page
interfaces.description: The requirement interacts with the user interface to display notification settings and receive user inputs.

test_cases.description: Toggle off 'likes' notifications and verify no notifications are received for likes.
test_cases.expected_result: User no longer receives notifications for likes when toggled off.

test_cases.description: Toggle on 'comments' notifications and verify notifications are received for comments.
test_cases.expected_result: User receives notifications for comments when toggled on.

requirements.title: Admin can grant verified badges on user profiles
requirements.description: The system shall allow an authenticated admin with user management privileges to grant a verified badge on a selected user profile.
requirements.rationale: To manage the verification status of users and ensure credibility within the system.
requirements.business_value: Improves user trust by clearly indicating verified users, which can enhance engagement and functionality.

source.description: As an admin I need to grant or revoke verified badges on user profiles So that I can manage the verification status of users to ensure credibility

acceptance_criteria: Given I am an authenticated admin with user management privileges, When I select a user profile and choose to grant a verified badge, Then the user's profile displays a verified badge.

Given I am an authenticated admin with user management privileges, When I select a user profile that currently has a verified badge and choose to revoke it, Then the verified badge is removed from the user's profile.
preconditions: The system must be operational and accessible by authenticated admins with user management privileges.
The selected user profile must exist in the system.
postconditions: The user's profile must reflect the change in verification status (granted or revoked badge).
Any relevant logs or audit trails must record the action taken by the admin.
functional_constraints: The badge grant and revoke functionality must be idempotent to avoid unintended side effects.
The system must handle concurrent requests from multiple admins efficiently.
security_requirements: All actions related to granting or revoking badges must be logged for audit purposes.
Access to the user management interface must be restricted to authenticated admins with appropriate privileges.

non_functional_requirements.performance: The badge grant and revoke operations must complete within 200ms under normal conditions.
non_functional_requirements.availability: The system must be available 99.9% of the time, with no more than one hour downtime per month.
non_functional_requirements.scalability: The system must support up to 1000 concurrent users managing badges without performance degradation.
non_functional_requirements.usability: Error messages and feedback should be clear and concise, guiding admins through the process.

edge_cases: What happens when an admin attempts to grant a badge to a non-existent user profile?
How does the system handle concurrent requests for granting or revoking badges by multiple admins?
What if the admin tries to revoke a badge that is not currently assigned to the user?
dependencies: User Management Interface (UMI) must be operational and accessible.
Audit logging system must be in place to record all actions related to badge grants and revocations.

interfaces.name: Admin User Management Interface
interfaces.description: The interface through which admins can select a user profile and choose to grant or revoke verified badges.

interfaces.name: User Database
interfaces.description: The database where user profiles are stored, including the verified badge status.

interfaces.name: Audit Logging API
interfaces.description: API used to log actions related to granting and revoking badges for audit purposes.

test_cases.description: Granting a verified badge to an existing user profile.
test_cases.expected_result: The user's profile displays the verified badge, and logs are recorded indicating the action.

test_cases.description: Revoking a verified badge from a user profile that has one.
test_cases.expected_result: The verified badge is removed from the user's profile, and logs are recorded indicating the action.

test_cases.description: Attempting to grant a badge to a non-existent user profile.
test_cases.expected_result: An error message is displayed, indicating that the user profile does not exist.

test_cases.description: Concurrent requests for granting badges by multiple admins.
test_cases.expected_result: Badge grants complete successfully without conflict, and logs are recorded for each action.

requirements.title: Create Group and Set Visibility
requirements.description: The system shall allow a user to create a group and select its visibility as public or private.
requirements.rationale: To enable users to control who can view or join their groups, ensuring that only authorized individuals have access.
requirements.business_value: Improves security and privacy by allowing users to restrict group visibility, reducing the risk of unauthorized access.

source.description: As a user I need to create a group and set its visibility as public or private So that I can control who can view or join the group

acceptance_criteria: The group is created with the specified visibility (public or private).
preconditions: A user is on the group creation page.
postconditions: The system creates a new group with the selected visibility.
Public groups are visible and joinable by anyone.
Private groups require approval or invitation.
security_requirements: Access control is enforced based on the group visibility setting (public/private).
edge_cases: What happens if a user tries to create a group without selecting a visibility?
How does the system handle duplicate group names when creating public and private groups?
dependencies: Group management module must be implemented.
User authentication and authorization system must be operational.

interfaces.name: GroupCreationAPI
interfaces.description: Interface for creating a new group with specified visibility.

interfaces.name: GroupDB
interfaces.description: Database where group details are stored, including visibility settings.

test_cases.description: User attempts to create a public group.
test_cases.expected_result: A new group is created with the 'public' visibility setting, visible and joinable by anyone.

test_cases.description: User attempts to create a private group.
test_cases.expected_result: A new group is created with the 'private' visibility setting, requiring approval or invitation to join.

test_cases.description: User tries to create a group without selecting a visibility.
test_cases.expected_result: The system displays an error message and prevents group creation until a valid visibility is selected.

requirements.title: Toggle Profile Visibility
requirements.description: The system shall allow a user to toggle their profile visibility between public and private.
requirements.rationale: To enable users to control who sees their profile, enhancing privacy and security settings.
requirements.business_value: Improves user trust and confidence in the platform by giving them greater control over their personal information.

source.description: As a user, I need to toggle my profile visibility between public and private so that I can control who sees my profile.

acceptance_criteria: Given I am on my profile settings page
When I toggle the profile visibility option
Then my profile visibility changes between public and private accordingly
preconditions: The user must be logged in to their account.
The profile settings page must be accessible.
postconditions: The user's profile visibility is updated to the selected state.
All users affected by the change (public to private) should no longer see the profile.
functional_constraints: The system must handle the toggle operation in less than 2 seconds.
security_requirements: Only authenticated users can access and modify their profile settings.
Profile visibility changes are logged for security audit purposes.

non_functional_requirements.performance: System response time to profile visibility toggle must be under 200ms.
non_functional_requirements.availability: The system must be available 99.9% of the time during peak usage periods.
non_functional_requirements.scalability: The system must support up to 1 million active users without significant performance degradation.
non_functional_requirements.usability: Error messages should be clear and actionable, guiding the user on how to resolve issues.

edge_cases: What happens if the user toggles their profile visibility to private and then immediately logs out and back in?
How does the system handle a scenario where there are network issues during the toggle operation?

interfaces.name: Profile Settings API
interfaces.description: The system interacts with this API to retrieve and update profile settings.

interfaces.name: User Profiles Database
interfaces.description: This database stores the visibility status of user profiles.

test_cases.description: Verify that toggling profile visibility to public from private is successful.
test_cases.expected_result: The user's profile should be visible to everyone after the toggle operation.

test_cases.description: Verify that toggling profile visibility to private from public is successful.
test_cases.expected_result: The user's profile should be visible only to the user or authorized connections after the toggle operation.

requirements.title: Send Friend Request
requirements.description: The system shall send a friend request to another user and notify the recipient.
requirements.rationale: To facilitate connections between users, allowing them to initiate social interactions within the platform.
requirements.business_value: Enhances user engagement by providing a means for users to connect with others, thereby increasing community interaction and satisfaction.

source.description: As a user, I need to send friend requests to other users and accept or reject incoming requests so that I can connect with others and manage my network.

acceptance_criteria: The recipient receives a notification about the pending friend request.
When accepted, the sender and receiver are both marked as friends, and each gets a notification of this change.
When rejected, the sender is notified that their request has been rejected.
preconditions: The user must be logged in to the system.
There should be another user available to send a friend request to.
postconditions: A new pending friend request is added to the recipient's list.
If accepted, a mutual friendship status is established between the sender and receiver.
If rejected, the request disappears from both users' lists of pending requests.
security_requirements: User identities must be verified before allowing friend requests to be sent or received.
All communications related to friend requests must be encrypted.

non_functional_requirements.performance: Friend request processing should occur within a reasonable time frame, typically less than 5 seconds.
non_functional_requirements.availability: The system must be available 99.9% of the time during business hours.
non_functional_requirements.scalability: The system should handle an increasing number of concurrent users and friend requests without performance degradation.
non_functional_requirements.usability: Error messages for failed requests should be clear and helpful, guiding the user on how to proceed.

edge_cases: What happens if the recipient is offline?
How does the system handle duplicate friend requests?
What should happen if a user tries to send a request to themselves?
What if there are network issues when sending or receiving notifications?
dependencies: Database for storing friendship statuses and requests

interfaces.name: Friendship API
interfaces.description: Interface for sending, accepting, and rejecting friend requests.

interfaces.name: User Database
interfaces.description: Stores user information and friendship statuses.

interfaces.name: Friend Request Notification
interfaces.description: Displays notifications to users about incoming friend requests.

test_cases.description: User sends a friend request to another user who is online.
test_cases.expected_result: The recipient receives a notification of the pending request.

test_cases.description: User tries to send a friend request to themselves.
test_cases.expected_result: The system rejects the request and notifies the user with an error message.

test_cases.description: User accepts a pending friend request from another user.
test_cases.expected_result: Both users are marked as friends, and each receives a notification of this change.

test_cases.description: User rejects a pending friend request from another user.
test_cases.expected_result: The request is removed, and the sender is notified that their request has been rejected.

requirements.title: Admin Actions Logging Requirement
requirements.description: The system shall log all admin actions (e.g., banning, warning) performed by administrators with details including the admin ID, action type, timestamp, and target user.
requirements.rationale: Logging of admin actions is necessary for auditing purposes to ensure accountability and traceability of system operations.
requirements.business_value: Enables compliance with regulatory requirements, provides a historical record of actions taken, and aids in incident investigation and resolution.

source.description: As a admin I need all admin actions (e.g., banning, warning) to be logged So that actions can be audited.

acceptance_criteria: Given an admin performs an action (e.g., banning or warning a user)
When the action is completed
Then the action is recorded in the audit log with details (admin ID, action type, timestamp, target user)
preconditions: Admin must have sufficient privileges to perform actions
postconditions: Action must be recorded in the audit log
Log must include admin ID, action type, timestamp, and target user details
functional_constraints: Audit log must be stored persistently
Logs must be accessible for review by authorized personnel

non_functional_requirements.performance: Log recording should be performed within 100ms of action completion
non_functional_requirements.scalability: System must handle up to 1,000 concurrent admin actions without performance degradation
non_functional_requirements.usability: Error messages must be clear and actionable

edge_cases: What happens if an admin performs an action without sufficient privileges?
How does the system handle extremely high volumes of concurrent admin actions?
If a user is warned multiple times, should each warning be logged separately?

interfaces.name: Admin Action API
interfaces.description: The system must provide an API for admin actions that will trigger the logging functionality.

interfaces.name: Audit Log Database
interfaces.description: The audit log data should be stored in a persistent database accessible by authorized personnel.

test_cases.description: Admin bans a user and the action is logged.
test_cases.expected_result: A new entry is added to the audit log with admin ID, 'BAN', timestamp, and target user details.

test_cases.description: Admin warns a user multiple times and each warning is logged separately.
test_cases.expected_result: Multiple entries are added to the audit log for each warning with admin ID, 'WARN', timestamp, and target user details.

test_cases.description: Admin performs an action without sufficient privileges.
test_cases.expected_result: The system should reject the action and log an error message indicating insufficient privileges.

requirements.title: User must be able to block another user to hide their posts and messages
requirements.description: The system shall allow a logged-in user to block another user, which shall hide all existing and future posts and messages from the blocked user from the viewer's view.
requirements.rationale: Blocking other users is necessary for maintaining privacy and avoiding unwanted content in the feed or inbox.
requirements.business_value: Improved user experience by reducing clutter and focusing on relevant content. Enhanced privacy control for users who prefer not to interact with certain individuals.

source.description: As a user I need to block another user So that their posts and messages are hidden from me

acceptance_criteria: All posts and messages from 'Alice' are hidden from my view
I no longer receive new messages or see new posts from 'Alice'
preconditions: I am a logged-in user
I have a list of posts and messages from user 'Alice'
postconditions: All existing and future posts and messages from 'Alice' are hidden
I no longer receive new messages or see new posts from 'Alice'
security_requirements: Blocking functionality should be secure against unauthorized access

non_functional_requirements.performance: No significant performance degradation when blocking a user
non_functional_requirements.availability: The system must be available during the entire process of blocking and unblocking users
non_functional_requirements.scalability: The system should handle an increase in the number of blocked users without issues
non_functional_requirements.usability: Error messages should be clear and actionable

edge_cases: Blocking a user who does not exist
Attempting to block oneself
Unblocking a user who was previously blocked
dependencies: User authentication system for identifying logged-in users
Data storage and retrieval for posts, messages, and user relationships
Real-time messaging system for notifications and updates

interfaces.name: User Profile API
interfaces.description: Provides access to user profiles and allows updating the block status.

interfaces.name: Posts Database
interfaces.description: Stores and retrieves posts, which need to be filtered based on the blocking status.

interfaces.name: Messages Database
interfaces.description: Stores and retrieves messages, which need to be filtered based on the blocking status.

interfaces.name: Notification System
interfaces.description: Handles sending notifications when a user is blocked or unblocked.

test_cases.description: Blocking a non-existing user.
test_cases.expected_result: The system should return an error message indicating the user does not exist.

test_cases.description: Attempting to block oneself.
test_cases.expected_result: The system should prevent blocking and return an error message.

test_cases.description: Unblocking a user who was previously blocked.
test_cases.expected_result: All posts and messages from the unblocked user should reappear in the view.

requirements.title: Create a User Profile
requirements.description: The system shall allow registered users to create a profile with a profile photo and a short biography.
requirements.rationale: Enabling users to create personalized profiles enhances user experience and facilitates social interaction within the platform.
requirements.business_value: Increased user engagement and satisfaction through personalization, potentially leading to higher retention rates and more active participation in community features.

source.description: As a registered user I need create a profile with a profile photo and a short biography So that I can personalize my account and share information about myself

acceptance_criteria: Given I am a registered user
When I upload a valid photo and enter a biography under 500 characters
Then my profile is created and displayed successfully
postconditions: A new profile is created with the provided photo and biography
The profile is displayed on the user's account settings page
functional_constraints: Profile creation requires a valid photo (JPEG, PNG) up to 2 MB
Profile can be created from the account settings page
Biography length should not exceed 500 characters
edge_cases: User uploads a photo larger than 2 MB
User enters a biography longer than 500 characters
User attempts to create a profile without uploading a photo or entering a biography

interfaces.name: Account Settings Page
interfaces.description: The requirement interacts with the UI of the account settings page where users can upload photos and enter biographies.

test_cases.description: User uploads a valid photo (JPEG, PNG) up to 2 MB and enters a biography under 500 characters.
test_cases.expected_result: The profile is created and displayed successfully.

test_cases.description: User attempts to upload a photo larger than 2 MB.
test_cases.expected_result: The system displays an error message indicating the file size limit has been exceeded.

test_cases.description: User enters a biography longer than 500 characters.
test_cases.expected_result: The system displays an error message indicating the biography length exceeds the limit.

test_cases.description: User attempts to create a profile without uploading a photo or entering a biography.
test_cases.expected_result: The system prompts the user to upload a photo and enter a biography before allowing profile creation.

requirements.title: User Follow/Unfollow Functionality
requirements.description: The system shall allow a logged-in user to follow or unfollow other users and update follower/following counts in real-time or on page refresh.
requirements.rationale: This feature is essential for managing social connections and understanding the influence of followed users.
requirements.business_value: Improves user engagement and provides insights into the popularity and influence of other users within the platform.

source.description: As a user I need to follow or unfollow other users and see the follower/following counts on their profiles so that I can manage my connections and understand their social influence.

acceptance_criteria: Given I am logged in and viewing another user's profile When I click the 'Follow' button Then the button changes to 'Unfollow' and the follower count on that profile increases by 1 And the user I followed sees a new follower notification (if applicable)
Given I am currently following a user When I click the 'Unfollow' button on their profile Then the button changes to 'Follow' and the follower count on that profile decreases by 1
preconditions: The user must be logged in
Another user's profile page is being viewed
postconditions: If following, the user should see an updated follower count of +1 on their profile and the target user's followers list should include the current user
If unfollowing, the user should see an updated follower count of -1 on their profile and the target user's followers list should no longer include the current user
functional_constraints: Follower and following counts must update in real-time or after a page refresh
The 'Follow' button text changes to 'Unfollow' when followed, and back to 'Follow' when unfollowed
security_requirements: Authentication is required before performing follow/unfollow actions

non_functional_requirements.performance: Response time for following/unfollowing should be less than 2 seconds
non_functional_requirements.availability: The system must be available at least 99.9% of the time
non_functional_requirements.scalability: The system must handle up to 100,000 concurrent users without performance degradation
non_functional_requirements.usability: Error messages should clearly indicate if follow/unfollow actions fail and provide guidance on how to resolve issues

edge_cases: What happens if a user tries to follow themselves?
How does the system handle network latency during follow/unfollow operations?
What if the target user's profile is private or deleted?
dependencies: Database for User Profiles and Relationships

interfaces.name: Follow/Unfollow API
interfaces.description: The system provides an API endpoint to handle follow/unfollow requests.

interfaces.name: User Profile Database
interfaces.description: Interacts with the database to update follower/following counts and manage user relationships.

test_cases.description: Verify that a logged-in user can follow another user.
test_cases.expected_result: The 'Follow' button changes to 'Unfollow', the follower count increases by 1, and the target user receives a notification.

test_cases.description: Verify that a logged-in user can unfollow another user.
test_cases.expected_result: The 'Unfollow' button changes to 'Follow', the follower count decreases by 1, and the target user is no longer in the follower list.

test_cases.description: Verify that a user cannot follow themselves.
test_cases.expected_result: The system should prevent following oneself and display an appropriate error message.

requirements.title: User can send and receive private messages
requirements.description: The system shall allow a logged-in user to initiate, compose, send, and receive private messages with another user.
requirements.rationale: Enables one-on-one communication between users, enhancing interaction and engagement within the platform.
requirements.business_value: Improves user experience and fosters direct communication, potentially increasing user satisfaction and community engagement.

source.description: As a user I need to send and receive private messages directly with another user so that I can communicate one-on-one with other users.

acceptance_criteria: Given I am a logged-in user, When I navigate to a user's profile and click 'Send Message', Then I can compose and send a private message to that user.
Given I have received a private message from another user, When I open my inbox, Then I can read the message and reply directly to the sender.
preconditions: User must be logged in to access messaging functionality.
postconditions: Private messages are sent successfully between two users.
Received messages are visible in the recipient's inbox.
Replying to a message updates the conversation history.
functional_constraints: Messages must be delivered instantly.
Only two participants should see the conversation thread.
security_requirements: Authentication and authorization mechanisms must ensure only authorized users can send or receive messages.
Data encryption must be used to protect message content during transmission and at rest.

non_functional_requirements.performance: Response time for sending and receiving messages should be less than 2 seconds.
non_functional_requirements.availability: System should be available 99.9% of the time.
non_functional_requirements.scalability: System must support thousands of concurrent users and tens of thousands of messages per day without performance degradation.
non_functional_requirements.usability: Error handling should provide clear, actionable feedback to users.

edge_cases: What happens if a user tries to send a message to an unregistered user?
How does the system handle large volumes of messages simultaneously?
What is the behavior when one of the participants leaves the conversation mid-message?
dependencies: Database for storing messages and user information

interfaces.name: Message Service API
interfaces.description: Interface for sending, receiving, and retrieving private messages.

interfaces.name: User Database
interfaces.description: Stores user information necessary for message delivery.

test_cases.description: Sending a private message from one user to another.
test_cases.expected_result: The message is sent successfully and appears in both users' inboxes.

test_cases.description: Receiving a private message and replying.
test_cases.expected_result: The received message is displayed, and the reply is sent to the sender's inbox.

requirements.title: User can like and unlike a post
requirements.description: The system shall allow users to click a like or unlike button on a post, which updates the total number of likes immediately and toggles the button state.
requirements.rationale: Enabling users to interact with content through liking allows for engagement and provides visibility into the popularity of posts, enhancing user experience and community interaction.
requirements.business_value: Increased engagement and visibility can lead to higher user retention and more interaction on the platform.

source.description: As a user I need to like or unlike a post and see the total number of likes displayed So that I can engage with content and view its popularity

acceptance_criteria: Given a post with an initial like count When I click the like button Then the like count increases by 1 And the button changes to an unliked state
Given a post I have already liked When I click the unlike button Then the like count decreases by 1 And the button changes back to a liked state
preconditions: The user must be logged in
The post must exist in the system
postconditions: The total like count on the post is updated immediately
The like/unlike button reflects the current state (liked or unliked)
functional_constraints: The like and unlike functionality must be toggleable
The like count must be displayed accurately

non_functional_requirements.performance: Response time for liking/unliking a post should be less than or equal to 500ms
non_functional_requirements.availability: The system should be available 99.9% of the time
non_functional_requirements.scalability: The system must be able to handle at least 1,000 posts and 10,000 users simultaneously
non_functional_requirements.usability: Error messages should be clear and user-friendly

edge_cases: What happens if a user tries to like/unlike a post that does not exist?
How does the system handle concurrent likes and unlikes from multiple users?
What if a user tries to like/unlike their own post?

interfaces.name: Like/Unlike API
interfaces.description: Interacts with the backend to update the like count and toggle the button state.

interfaces.name: Post Database
interfaces.description: Stores the current like count for each post.

test_cases.description: A user logs in and likes a post with an initial like count of 0.
test_cases.expected_result: The like count increases to 1, and the like button changes to an unliked state.

test_cases.description: A user logs in and likes a post they have already liked.
test_cases.expected_result: The like count remains unchanged, and the like button changes back to a liked state.

test_cases.description: Multiple users like and unlike a single post simultaneously.
test_cases.expected_result: The like count is accurately updated for each user's action, and the buttons reflect their current states correctly.

requirements.title: User and Post Search Functionality
requirements.description: The system shall allow a logged-in user to search across all users and posts using keywords, displaying a combined list of matching users and posts with type labels.
requirements.rationale: Enabling quick search capabilities will improve user engagement by facilitating easy discovery of relevant content and individuals within the platform.
requirements.business_value: This functionality will enhance user satisfaction and productivity by enabling faster access to information, potentially leading to increased user retention and higher engagement rates.

source.description: As a user I need to search across all users and posts using keywords So that I can quickly find relevant users or content

acceptance_criteria: The search bar must accept and process keywords.
The system must return a list of users and posts that match the keyword.
The list must include type labels for each result to distinguish between users and posts.
preconditions: A user must be logged into the system.
postconditions: A list of matching users and posts must be displayed on the screen.
functional_constraints: The search functionality must be case-insensitive.
Search results must include relevant posts and users within a reasonable time frame.

non_functional_requirements.performance: The search should complete within 2 seconds for at least 95% of queries.
non_functional_requirements.availability: The system must be available 99.9% of the time during normal operations.
non_functional_requirements.scalability: The system must support at least 10,000 concurrent users without significant performance degradation.
non_functional_requirements.usability: Error messages must be clear and actionable.

edge_cases: Empty search keyword
Search keyword containing special characters
User with no name or description
Post with no content
dependencies: Database to store users and posts

interfaces.name: Search API
interfaces.description: Provides an endpoint to receive search keywords and return matching users and posts.

interfaces.name: User Database
interfaces.description: Stores user information and their searchable descriptions.

interfaces.name: Post Database
interfaces.description: Stores post content and their searchable keywords.

test_cases.description: User searches for an existing user name
test_cases.expected_result: The system displays the matching user in the search results with a 'User' type label.

test_cases.description: User searches for a non-existent keyword
test_cases.expected_result: The system displays an empty list or a message indicating no matches found.

test_cases.description: Post contains the search keyword
test_cases.expected_result: The system displays the matching post in the search results with a 'Post' type label.

requirements.title: User should be able to bookmark posts and view them later
requirements.description: The system shall allow a user to mark a post as bookmarked.
requirements.rationale: This requirement enables users to save posts they find interesting for later access, enhancing the overall usability and satisfaction of the platform.
requirements.business_value: Improved user experience by providing easy access to previously saved content, leading to increased engagement and time spent on the platform.

source.description: As a user I need to bookmark posts and view them later in a saved list So that I can easily access and revisit posts I find interesting

acceptance_criteria: Given I am logged in as a user
When I bookmark a post
Then the post should be added to my saved list
Given I have bookmarked posts
When I navigate to my saved list
Then I should see all my bookmarked posts
preconditions: User is logged in
postconditions: Post is added to the user's saved list
edge_cases: User logs out while trying to bookmark a post
Post is deleted after being bookmarked by the user

interfaces.name: User Interface
interfaces.description: The requirement interacts with the user interface to allow users to mark posts as bookmarked and access their saved list.

interfaces.name: User Database
interfaces.description: The requirement interacts with a database to store and retrieve the list of bookmarked posts for each user.

test_cases.description: User bookmarks a post while logged in.
test_cases.expected_result: Post is added to the user's saved list.

test_cases.description: User attempts to bookmark a post while not logged in.
test_cases.expected_result: System displays an error message and prevents the post from being bookmarked.

test_cases.description: User navigates to their saved list after bookmarking several posts.
test_cases.expected_result: All bookmarked posts are displayed in the saved list.

requirements.title: View Post History
requirements.description: The system shall display a list of the user's previous posts with timestamps on their profile page.
requirements.rationale: Enabling users to view their post history allows them to track and manage their content more effectively.
requirements.business_value: Improves user satisfaction by providing transparency into past actions, facilitating better decision-making.

source.description: As a user I need to view my own post history and manage account settings (change password, email, delete account) So that I can control my account and review my posts

acceptance_criteria: The list of previous posts shall be displayed on the profile page.
Each post in the list shall include a timestamp.
Users shall have access to account settings from their profile page.
The change password option shall allow users to update their password securely.
The change email option shall allow users to update their email address and verify it.
The delete account option shall allow users to irreversible delete their account with a confirmation step.
preconditions: The user must be logged in to access the profile page.
The system must have access to the user's post history data.
The system must be able to retrieve and display data from the database.
postconditions: Users shall see a list of their previous posts with timestamps on their profile page.
User password, email, or account deletion request shall be processed securely.
The user's account information shall be updated accordingly.
functional_constraints: The system shall handle large volumes of post data without performance degradation.
Data retrieval and display operations shall be optimized for speed.
security_requirements: All password changes shall be encrypted before storage.
Email verification shall be implemented to prevent unauthorized access.
Account deletion request must require a second step for confirmation.

non_functional_requirements.performance: The system shall respond within 2 seconds to post history and account settings requests.
non_functional_requirements.availability: The system shall be available 99.9% of the time.
non_functional_requirements.scalability: The system shall support up to 10,000 users simultaneously without performance degradation.
non_functional_requirements.usability: Error messages shall clearly explain issues and provide actionable steps for resolution.

edge_cases: What happens when a user tries to delete their account and later decides not to?
How does the system handle concurrent updates to a user's account settings?
dependencies: Database for storing user post history and account information
Authentication service for verifying user credentials

interfaces.name: User Profile API
interfaces.description: Provides endpoints for retrieving a user's post history and accessing account settings.

interfaces.name: Post History Database
interfaces.description: Stores the user's posts along with timestamps.

test_cases.description: Verify that a user can view their post history after logging in.
test_cases.expected_result: The system displays a list of the user's previous posts with timestamps on their profile page.

test_cases.description: Test changing the password through the account settings.
test_cases.expected_result: The user's password is updated securely, and they are prompted to verify the new password.

test_cases.description: Verify that deleting an account requires a second step for confirmation.
test_cases.expected_result: The system prompts the user for confirmation before deleting their account, and the account is deleted only upon successful confirmation.

requirements.title: User should be able to report a post by selecting a reason from a dropdown menu
requirements.description: The system shall allow a logged-in user to report a post by selecting a reason from a dropdown menu and confirming the report.
requirements.rationale: This requirement enables users to flag inappropriate content for review, ensuring the integrity of the platform's content.
requirements.business_value: Improves the quality and safety of the platform's content by allowing timely review of flagged posts.

source.description: As a user, I need to report a post by selecting a reason from a dropdown menu so that inappropriate content can be flagged and reviewed.

acceptance_criteria: A dropdown menu with predefined report reasons is provided.
The user selects a reason and confirms the report.
The post is flagged for review.
A confirmation message is shown.
preconditions: The user must be logged in to report a post.
postconditions: The selected report reason is recorded for the post.
The post status is updated to 'flagged for review'.
A confirmation message is displayed to the user indicating that their report has been submitted.
security_requirements: User authentication must be required to submit a report.

non_functional_requirements.performance: Response time for submitting a report should not exceed 500ms.
non_functional_requirements.availability: The system must be available 99.9% of the time.
non_functional_requirements.scalability: The system should handle up to 1,000 reports per minute during peak hours.
non_functional_requirements.usability: Error messages should be clear and user-friendly.

edge_cases: User tries to report a post without being logged in.
Dropdown menu is empty or does not contain any options.
User selects an invalid reason from the dropdown menu.

interfaces.name: Post Report UI
interfaces.description: The user interface where users can select a reason to report a post and confirm the action.

interfaces.name: Content Moderation API
interfaces.description: The API that receives the report, flags the post for review, and updates the post status.

test_cases.description: User tries to report a post without being logged in.
test_cases.expected_result: System displays an error message prompting the user to log in and prevents the report from being submitted.

test_cases.description: Dropdown menu is empty or does not contain any options.
test_cases.expected_result: System displays an error message indicating that the dropdown menu is unavailable and prevents the report from being submitted.

test_cases.description: User selects an invalid reason from the dropdown menu.
test_cases.expected_result: System displays an error message indicating that the selected reason is invalid and prevents the report from being submitted.

test_cases.description: User successfully reports a post with a valid reason.
test_cases.expected_result: Post is flagged for review, system updates the post status to 'flagged for review', and displays a confirmation message to the user.

requirements.title: Add, edit, and delete comments on posts
requirements.description: The system shall allow users to add comments to posts and manage their own comments by editing or deleting them.
requirements.rationale: To enable user engagement with content and provide control over their contributions, allowing for interaction and feedback on platform posts.
requirements.business_value: Improves user experience by enabling active participation in community discussions and personal contribution management.

source.description: As a user I need add comments on posts and manage my own comments (edit/delete) So that I can engage with content and control my contributions

acceptance_criteria: Given I am on a post detail page When I write a comment and submit Then the comment appears on the post
Given I have a comment on a post When I edit my comment Then the comment content is updated
Given I have a comment on a post When I delete my comment Then the comment is removed from the post
preconditions: The user must be logged in to the platform.
A post detail page must be accessible and contain comments section.
postconditions: A new comment must appear on the post immediately after submission.
Editing a comment must update the comment content in real-time.
Deleting a comment must remove it from the post without leaving any trace.
functional_constraints: Comments must be flat, no nesting allowed.
The system must ensure that only the owner of a comment can edit or delete it.
security_requirements: User authentication and authorization mechanisms must be in place to prevent unauthorized editing or deletion of comments.

non_functional_requirements.performance: Comment submission, editing, and deletion should complete within 2 seconds.
non_functional_requirements.availability: The system must be available 99.5% of the time during peak usage hours.
non_functional_requirements.scalability: The system must support at least 10,000 concurrent users without performance degradation.
non_functional_requirements.usability: Error messages must be clear and actionable to help users understand what went wrong.

edge_cases: What happens if a user tries to edit or delete a comment that does not belong to them?
How does the system handle extremely long comments (e.g., over 10,000 characters)?
What is the behavior when the system reaches its concurrent user limit during peak hours?

interfaces.name: Comment Management API
interfaces.description: Interacts with the application's backend to manage comments, including creation, editing, and deletion.

interfaces.name: Post Detail Page UI
interfaces.description: Provides a platform for users to view posts and interact by adding, editing, or deleting comments.

test_cases.description: User can successfully submit a comment on a post.
test_cases.expected_result: The new comment appears on the post immediately after submission.

test_cases.description: Comment owner can edit their own comment.
test_cases.expected_result: Editing the comment updates its content in real-time.

test_cases.description: Non-owner user tries to edit another user's comment.
test_cases.expected_result: The system denies access and shows an error message.

test_cases.description: User can delete their own comment.
test_cases.expected_result: Deleting the comment removes it from the post without leaving any trace.

requirements.title: Group Owner/Admin Join Request Management
requirements.description: The system shall allow group owners/admins to review and approve/reject join requests for private groups.
requirements.rationale: To ensure that only approved members can join private groups, thereby maintaining the integrity and security of the group's content and activities.
requirements.business_value: Improves control over group membership, enhances group management capabilities, and ensures compliance with group policies regarding who is allowed to participate.

source.description: As a group owner/admin I need to review and approve/reject join requests for private groups So that only approved members can join the private group

acceptance_criteria: A private group has pending join requests.
The group owner/admin accesses the join requests management section.
They can see a list of pending requests with user details.
For each request, they can choose to approve or reject.
After approval, the requesting user becomes a member of the group.
After rejection, the request is removed and the user is not added.
preconditions: The system is running and accessible.
The user is authenticated as a group owner/admin.
postconditions: Pending join requests are either approved or rejected.
Approved users are added to the private group.
Rejected requests are removed from the list.
functional_constraints: Join request management must be available 24/7.
Response time for approving/rejecting a request should be less than 5 seconds.
security_requirements: Access to join request management is restricted to group owners/admins.
User details in pending requests must be encrypted at rest and in transit.

non_functional_requirements.performance: Response time for approving/rejecting a request should be less than 5 seconds.
non_functional_requirements.availability: The system must be available 99.9% of the time.
non_functional_requirements.scalability: The system must be able to handle at least 100 concurrent join requests without performance degradation.
non_functional_requirements.usability: Error messages should be clear and actionable.

edge_cases: What happens when a user tries to approve/reject a request that has already been handled?
How does the system handle concurrent approval/rejection requests from different administrators?
What if there are no pending join requests?
What happens if the group is deleted while a request is being processed?

interfaces.name: Join Requests Database
interfaces.description: Stores pending join requests and their status.

interfaces.name: Group API
interfaces.description: Used to add or remove users from a group.

test_cases.description: Verify that the system displays a list of pending join requests with user details.
test_cases.expected_result: A table is displayed showing all pending requests, including user names and request dates.

test_cases.description: Verify that an administrator can approve a pending join request.
test_cases.expected_result: The requesting user's status changes from 'Pending' to 'Approved', and they are added to the group.

test_cases.description: Verify that an administrator can reject a pending join request.
test_cases.expected_result: The requesting user's status changes from 'Pending' to 'Rejected', and their request is removed.

requirements.title: Display News Feed Sorted by Time
requirements.description: The system shall display a list of posts from the user's friends on the news feed page, sorted by creation time in descending order.
requirements.rationale: Users need to see their friends' recent activities without missing any updates, and this feature ensures they can quickly access new content from people they follow.
requirements.business_value: Increased engagement and satisfaction with the platform as users can stay up-to-date on friends' activities efficiently.

source.description: As a user, I need to see a news feed showing posts from my friends in chronological order so that I can view my friends' recent activities without missing any updates.

acceptance_criteria: The user sees a list of posts from friends sorted by time (newest first).
preconditions: The user is logged in.
The user has friends with posts.
functional_constraints: Posts are displayed only from friends of the user.
Posts are sorted by creation time, with the newest appearing first.
edge_cases: What happens when a user has no friends with posts?
How does the system handle new posts from friends added after the feed is loaded?
What if a friend deletes their post while the user is viewing the news feed?

interfaces.name: Friendship API
interfaces.description: The requirement interacts with this API to retrieve the list of friends for the authenticated user.

interfaces.name: Post Database
interfaces.description: The requirement retrieves posts from this database, filtered by the authenticated user's friends and sorted by creation time.

test_cases.description: User with no friends should see an empty news feed.
test_cases.expected_result: The news feed displays no posts.

test_cases.description: User with one friend and one post from that friend.
test_cases.expected_result: The news feed shows the single post in descending order of creation time.

test_cases.description: User adds a new friend, who posts immediately after adding the user to their friends list.
test_cases.expected_result: The new post from the added friend appears at the top of the news feed.

test_cases.description: User views the news feed while a friend's post is being deleted.
test_cases.expected_result: The news feed does not show the deleted post, and any existing posts remain sorted correctly.

requirements.title: Admin Reported Content Panel
requirements.description: The system shall provide an admin panel to review reported content and take appropriate actions such as warn, hide, or delete.
requirements.rationale: This requirement is necessary for effective content moderation by admins, ensuring that harmful or inappropriate content can be managed promptly.
requirements.business_value: Effective moderation leads to a safer user experience and maintains the integrity of the platform's content.

source.description: As an admin, I need a panel to review reported content and take appropriate actions so that I can moderate content effectively.

acceptance_criteria: I see a list of reported items with options to warn, hide, or delete
preconditions: I am logged in as an admin
security_requirements: Admin must be authenticated
edge_cases: What happens if there are no reported items?
How should the system handle actions that result in errors (e.g., deleting a non-existent item)?
What if an admin tries to perform an action without sufficient permissions?

interfaces.name: Admin Reported Content Panel
interfaces.description: The user interface for admins to view and manage reported content.

interfaces.name: Content Management Database
interfaces.description: Stores information about reported content, including the items to be moderated and their current status.

test_cases.description: Admin logs in and navigates to the reported content panel.
test_cases.expected_result: The admin sees a list of reported items with options to warn, hide, or delete.

test_cases.description: Admin selects an item to warn.
test_cases.expected_result: The system warns the user who posted the content and marks it as reviewed.

test_cases.description: Admin selects an item to hide.
test_cases.expected_result: The system hides the content from public view but retains it for further review.

test_cases.description: Admin attempts to perform an action without being authenticated.
test_cases.expected_result: The system denies access and prompts the admin to log in.

requirements.title: User receives notification on post interactions
requirements.description: The system shall notify a user via email, push, or in-app when someone likes or comments on their published post.
requirements.rationale: To keep users informed about interactions with their content, ensuring they stay engaged and connected with their audience.
requirements.business_value: Increased engagement from followers by promptly receiving notifications of likes and comments, potentially leading to more interaction and community building.

source.description: As a user, I need to receive notifications when someone likes or comments on my post so that I can stay updated on interactions with my post.

acceptance_criteria: Given I am a logged-in user with a published post, When another user likes my post, Then I receive a notification that someone liked my post.
Given I am a logged-in user with a published post, When another user comments on my post, Then I receive a notification that someone commented on my post.
Given I am a logged-in user with a published post, When I have disabled notifications for that post, And another user likes or comments on my post, Then I do not receive any notification.
preconditions: The user must be logged in to the system
The user's account must have notifications enabled for post interactions
The post must be published and accessible to other users
postconditions: The user receives a notification if their post is liked or commented on by another user
No notification is received if the user has disabled notifications for that specific post
functional_constraints: Notifications may be delivered via email, push, or in-app
The system must track likes and comments on posts
The system must differentiate between types of notifications (likes vs. comments)
security_requirements: User authentication is required to receive notifications
Notifications should not contain sensitive user information

non_functional_requirements.performance: Notification delivery within a maximum time frame
non_functional_requirements.availability: High availability of the notification system
non_functional_requirements.scalability: The system must handle an increasing number of users and posts without performance degradation
non_functional_requirements.usability: Notifications should be easily visible and understandable to the user

edge_cases: What happens if a user has multiple devices and notifications are enabled on all of them?
How does the system handle high traffic periods when many users interact with posts?
What if a user changes their notification preferences while an interaction is being processed?

interfaces.name: Notification Service API
interfaces.description: The requirement interacts with this API to send notifications via email, push, or in-app.

interfaces.name: User Preferences Database
interfaces.description: The requirement reads from and writes to this database to determine if notifications are enabled for specific posts.

test_cases.description: A logged-in user receives a notification when another user likes their post.
test_cases.expected_result: The system sends a notification to the user's preferred method (email, push, or in-app) confirming that someone liked their post.

test_cases.description: A logged-in user receives a notification when another user comments on their post.
test_cases.expected_result: The system sends a notification to the user's preferred method (email, push, or in-app) confirming that someone commented on their post.

test_cases.description: A logged-in user does not receive a notification when another user likes or comments on their post if notifications are disabled.
test_cases.expected_result: The system does not send any notification to the user, and no record of attempted notification is created.

requirements.title: Group Post Feed Filter
requirements.description: The system shall filter the group post feed to display only posts from groups the user belongs to.
requirements.rationale: To ensure that users see relevant content and are not exposed to unrelated posts, improving their experience and engagement within the system.
requirements.business_value: Enhances user satisfaction by providing a more personalized and focused view of content, leading to higher retention rates and increased user interaction with the platform.

source.description: As a group member I need to see a feed of posts only from the groups I belong to so that I can stay updated with content from my groups without unrelated posts.

acceptance_criteria: I see only posts from the groups I belong to
I do not see posts from groups I am not a member of
preconditions: I am a group member logged into the system
I belong to multiple groups
functional_constraints: Posts are associated with specific groups
Feed is filtered based on user's group membership
security_requirements: User authentication must be in place
Access control to posts based on group membership

non_functional_requirements.performance: The feed should load within 2 seconds under normal conditions
non_functional_requirements.scalability: The system must support up to 10,000 active users and groups
non_functional_requirements.usability: Error messages should be clear and actionable

edge_cases: What happens if a user joins a new group after viewing the feed?
How does the system handle large groups with a high volume of posts?

interfaces.name: Group API
interfaces.description: The requirement interacts with this API to retrieve the list of groups a user belongs to.

interfaces.name: Post Database
interfaces.description: The requirement retrieves posts from this database, filtering them based on group membership.

test_cases.description: User sees only posts from groups they belong to after joining a new group.
test_cases.expected_result: The feed updates to include posts from the newly joined group.

test_cases.description: System handles large groups with 1,000 posts without performance degradation.
test_cases.expected_result: The feed loads within 2 seconds.
